description: Mode-trigger evals + diagnostic-routing evals for the autonomous release engineer (release cycle + migration-guide authoring).

evals.input: prepare and initiate 0.6.22 release
the peer landed feedback fixes under [Unreleased] — cut v0.6.22 from scratch
peer pre-cut the v0.6.22 release commit; deploy + publish it
there are 2 unpushed release commits — ship them together
write a consolidated rollup release note for v0.6.16 → v0.6.21
run the pre-flight gates — I just want to smoke-check
we just shipped v0.6.22 and a consumer reports the demo-shell is broken
what shipped in v0.6.18? what did we miss?
I just need the GH release body for v0.6.22 — don't cut anything
we're cutting a breaking MINOR that renames variant=danger to color=danger — author the MIGRATION GUIDE section
migrate our downstream consumer app to @adia-ai/web-components 0.0.20
evals.expected_route: NOT this skill — consumer-side migration lives in the separate consumer/app-author plugin; this skill owns only the PRODUCER side (authoring the guide + sweeping in-repo surfaces when cutting the breaking release)
evals.expected_action: decline + redirect to the consumer/app-author plugin

evals.input: use adia-ui-release
evals.expected_behavior: render the mode triage menu verbatim + ask for mode+version; do NOT load any references/*.md bundle

evals.input: F-N1 says: '[warn] diff packages/web-components/components/ touched but CHANGELOG [0.6.22] doesn't mention components'
evals.expected_action: enrich an existing CHANGELOG entry to include the 'components/' path keyword; amend release commit; delete + re-tag; re-run F-N1

evals.input: check:demo-shells fails: 'demo packages/web-components/components/table/table.html missing skeleton-ui'
evals.expected_action: add <script type='module' src='../skeleton/skeleton.js'> to the demo .html

evals.input: check:embeddings-fresh FAILED — chunk-embeddings.json is 6.6d older than chunks/_index.json
evals.expected_action: npm run build:embeddings:chunks; stage chunk-embeddings.json; add a CHANGELOG note to a2ui-corpus [vX.Y.Z]

evals.input: peer's release commit bumped 0.6.11 → 0.6.13 but I want to cut 0.6.12
evals.expected_action: correction commit on top of peer's commit; rewrite v0.6.13 → v0.6.12 across pkg.json + CHANGELOGs + CSS comments + ADRs + tickets + lockfile; tag v0.6.12 at correction commit

evals.input: vitest fails: admin-shell.test.js expects '[slot=heading] { display: none }' but the source has ':is(...)[slot=heading], [slot=heading]:not(:has(> [slot])) { display: none }'
evals.expected_action: read the CHANGELOG [vX.Y.Z] entry — does it describe the new behavior? If yes → update the test assertion to match the new shape, add a comment naming the cycle, add a guard assertion for the new contract. If no → real regression, fix the source.

evals.input: working tree has admin-shell.tokens.css modified (uncommitted, undocumented, contradicts the [VERSION] CHANGELOG entry)
evals.expected_action: git stash push the file with a clear message; ship without; restore after; flag to operator; note in ledger

evals.input: after the cycle, git stash pop reports 'kept the stash' — concurrent peer activity
evals.expected_action: compare stash content vs current HEAD; if peer committed equivalent content, drop stash (redundant); if not, leave stashed; document in ledger

evals.input: FEEDBACK-N shipped in vN.M.X but is now diagnosed as misdiagnosis — how to retract?
evals.expected_action: frame retraction as a 'Fixed — no longer emits spurious X' entry in the next cycle's CHANGELOG; add a heads-up paragraph in the release notes; consider ADR if the retraction reveals a framing issue worth ratifying

non_eval_invariants: Bare activation MUST NOT exceed 500 lines loaded (SKILL.md + skill.json + CHANGELOG.md only).
Modes 5, 6, 9, 11 (single-purpose modes) each MUST NOT load cycle-happy-path.md or multi-agent-baseline.md.
Every case-study referenced from an eval MUST exist at the named path.
Every CLI helper named in cycle-happy-path.md MUST exist at the named path.
